name: Claiming Hearing Loss from Previous Employers in Utah

mainEntity.name: Can I claim hearing loss from a previous employer under Utah workers' comp law?
mainEntity.text: Can I claim hearing loss from a previous employer under Utah workers' comp law?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, Utah Code 34A-2-504 limits employer liability to hearing loss occurring during employment with that specific employer. Prior occupational or nonoccupational loss, proven by evidence, is not compensable against the current employer.

suggestedAnswer.text: Utah law holds each employer responsible only for incremental hearing damage during their employment. Pre-existing loss from past jobs or non-work causes must be documented via baseline audiograms to deduct from claims. This 'successive injury' rule complicates multi-employer cases; apportionment requires expert testimony. Workers with long careers in noisy industries often face reduced awards—legal expertise is essential.[4]
